items.title: Overview
items.summary: Top-level introduction to the secured demo application.
items.detail: This landing area will evolve into the authenticated feature overview. AP3 uses it to prove that a JWT-backed session can unlock a shared application shell.

items.title: Bootstrap & Configuration
items.summary: createServer() bootstrap, config loading, and static delivery.
items.detail: The server already bootstraps expresto-server from JSON config and serves the React build. Later packages will expose the full configuration walkthrough here.

items.title: Controllers & Routing
items.summary: File-based controllers and route security modes.
items.detail: This page is planned for AP6 and will show public, Basic-protected, and JWT-protected handlers with source snippets.

items.title: Security
items.summary: Basic Auth, JWT generation, and session verification.
items.detail: AP3 activates the first part of this feature. The page itself will later show the complete request flow and validation examples.

items.title: Lifecycle Hooks
items.summary: Startup, middleware, and shutdown extension points.
items.detail: The navigation entry already exists so the protected app shell matches the target feature map. Hook-specific UI content follows in AP6.

items.title: Service Registry
items.summary: Shared infrastructure services and lifecycle management.
items.detail: A dedicated page for service registration and inspection will be added when the central service demos are introduced.

items.title: Event System
items.summary: Framework events, application events, and EventBus flows.
items.detail: The EventBus page is queued for AP6 and AP7, where it will become interactive through button-driven demo messages.

items.title: Scheduler
items.summary: Cron-driven background jobs inside the runtime.
items.detail: The scheduler page will later stream a server-side clock into the UI every ten seconds via the WebSocket demo.

items.title: WebSocket
items.summary: JWT-protected real-time transport on the shared server.
items.detail: WebSocket UI and live transport start in AP7. For now, the menu already reserves the protected navigation target.

items.title: Metrics & Observability
items.detail: This page will collect the /__metrics endpoint, request tracing notes, and runtime health references in a later package.

items.title: Public API & Contracts
items.summary: Stable exports, types, and supported v1 contracts.
items.detail: A reference-focused page for the documented public API is planned for AP8.
